instructions.primary_goal:
Issue: POL-437 — POL-437
Worktree: .
Branch: pol-434-delivery

## Goal
## Objective

Give every Polaris-generated `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` a marked, safely-regenerable region, and remove the duplicate `SUMMARY.md` scaffold template.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found two gaps that block safe reconciliation:

1. The only lifecycle marker today is whole-file (`DRAFT_MARKER`, `<!-- polaris:draft -->`, `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts:7`) — once a human removes the marker, the entire file becomes off-limits to any future automated update, even if most of its content is still boilerplate the tool generated. There is no way to regenerate just the machine-owned part of an already-reviewed file.
2. Two independent `SUMMARY.md` scaffold templates exist: `generateSummaryDraft()` in `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts` and `scaffoldDraftSummaryFiles()` in `src/cli/adopt-canon.ts:32-46` (a much more minimal template: `"# ${folder}\n\n<!-- polaris:draft -->\n"`). They produce different output for the same conceptual operation depending on which code path runs first.

Also relevant: sampled route-local files show an organically-added, non-canonical 8th "## Route model" section in deeper `src/` folders (e.g. `src/loop/POLARIS.md:40-45`, `src/cli/POLARIS.md`) that isn't part of the 7-section template defined in `smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md:28-127`. This child's template work must explicitly decide whether "Route model" becomes an optional canonical 8th section or is left as free-form content outside the GENERATED region — it must not silently strip existing content.

## Goal

1. `generateDraft()` and `generateSummaryDraft()` (`seed-instructions.ts`) wrap their machine-populated body content in `<!-- BEGIN POLARIS GENERATED -->` / `<!-- END POLARIS GENERATED -->` markers.
2. `adopt-canon.ts` calls the shared `seed-instructions.ts` summary/POLARIS template functions instead of maintaining its own `scaffoldDraftSummaryFiles()`.
3. `smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md` is updated to document the GENERATED-region convention and to explicitly rule on the "Route model" section (canonize as optional 8th section, or document it as allowed free-form content outside the GENERATED region).
4. `validate-instructions.ts`'s role-boundary/section checks are updated so they do not misfire on content inside vs. outside the GENERATED region.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-canon.ts
* src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.test.ts
* smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generated `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` content is wrapped in `<!-- BEGIN POLARIS GENERATED -->` / `<!-- END POLARIS GENERATED -->`
- [ ] `adopt-canon.ts` no longer defines its own SUMMARY.md scaffold template; it calls the shared generator
- [ ] Doctrine doc updated with the GENERATED-region convention and an explicit ruling on the "Route model" section
- [ ] `validate-instructions.ts` section/role-boundary checks pass on files containing GENERATED markers plus non-canonical trailing sections outside the markers
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of all other children in this cluster. Two sibling children (template-version fingerprint, one-shot reconciliation sweep) depend on this one.

## Non-goals

* Do not retrofit GENERATED markers onto already-promoted, hand-authored POLARIS.md files (that is the one-shot sweep child's job).
* Do not change the LLM prompt content in `adopt-canon.ts`'s `dispatchCanonAgent`.
* Do not add fingerprint/version stamping (covered by a sibling child).

## Implementation Discipline
Before writing code, stop at the first rung that holds:

1. Does this need to exist?   → no: skip it (YAGNI)
2. Stdlib does it?            → use it
3. Native platform feature?   → use it
4. Installed dependency?      → use it
5. One line?                  → one line
6. Only then: the minimum that works

Lazy, not negligent: validation, error handling, security, and accessibility are never on the chopping block.

When you defer a simplification for later, mark it inline:
  // ponytail: <what you deferred and why>

## Scope
Touch only:
- src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
- src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts
- src/cli/adopt-canon.ts
- src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts
- src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.ts
- src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.test.ts
- smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md

## Acceptance Criteria

## Validation
- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts

## Commit
[POL-437] POL-437

## Governance
- Do NOT modify open_children or completed_children in /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/current-state.json. The parent runtime (loop continue) owns state transitions.
- Append a telemetry event to /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/polaris-run-pol-434-2026-07-12-001/telemetry.jsonl.

## Worker Progress Telemetry (MANDATORY)
The parent CANNOT see your work. You MUST emit heartbeat events so the parent knows you are alive and making progress.

After EVERY step, append a JSONL event to /Users/lsctech/Developer/Polaris/.taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/runs/polaris-run-pol-434-2026-07-12-001/telemetry.jsonl:
```jsonl
{"event":"worker-heartbeat","run_id":"<from packet>","child_id":"POL-437","step_cursor":"<current step>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>","progress_pct":<0-100>,"files_changed":<count>,"current_file":"<file being edited>"}
```

Required heartbeat points:
  1. Immediately after reading this packet (step_cursor: "start")
  2. After verifying state file (step_cursor: "verify")
  3. After EACH file edit (step_cursor: "implement")
  4. After running validation (step_cursor: "validate")
  5. After creating commit (step_cursor: "commit")
  6. Before terminating (step_cursor: "complete" or "failed")

If heartbeats stop, the parent will assume you crashed and may dispatch a replacement worker.

## Approval Escalation (when uncertain)
You have a sealed packet defining your exact scope, allowed files, and validation commands. Use this to decide:

Rule 1 - Auto-approve WITHIN scope (normal work):
  - If the operation touches files within `instructions.allowed_scope` → Just do it
  - If the command is in `instructions.validation_commands` → Just run it
  - Low risk, expected work → No need to ask, emit heartbeat with step_cursor: "implement"

Rule 2 - ESCALATE out-of-scope or high-risk:
  - File outside allowed_scope → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "out-of-scope"
  - Destructive operation (rm -rf, major refactor) → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "destructive"
  - External API calls with cost → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "cost"
  - Changing secrets/.env → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "security"
  - Unclear requirements → Emit worker-blocked with approval_type: "ambiguous"

To escalate, emit IMMEDIATELY to telemetry:
```jsonl
{"event":"worker-blocked","run_id":"<from packet>","child_id":"POL-437","reason":"needs-approval","approval_type":"<out-of-scope|destructive|cost|security|ambiguous>","description":"<specifically what needs approval>","blocker_id":"<unique-id>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>"}
```

Then: Continue heartbeats every 30s with step_cursor: "blocked-awaiting-approval"
Wait for parent response. If no response in 5 minutes → TERMINATE with approval-timeout.

Remember: Your packet scope is your authority. Within scope = proceed. Outside scope = escalate.

## Route Cognition Delta
After implementation, apply route-local cognition delta — only if something materially changed.

**POLARIS.md** — update only when ALL of the following are true:
  - You touched a file in that folder (not a distant folder).
  - The change materially affects: folder responsibilities, commands/workflows,
    execution constraints, ownership/routing, or operational behavior.
  - The current POLARIS.md content is actually wrong or incomplete as a result.
  DO NOT update POLARIS.md for: formatting fixes, comment changes, tiny refactors,
  internal implementation details, or any change that leaves the operational guidance
  still accurate. When in doubt, do not update.

**SUMMARY.md** — update only when: linked docs/specs changed, canon relationships
  changed, architecture meaning changed, or doctrine/spec linkage changed.
  SUMMARY.md is informational only. Do not create it if missing — that is a hint for
  operators, not a task for workers. Keep it short. Never add operational doctrine.

**Never** scan unrelated folders. **Never** regenerate all route docs.
If no material change occurred, skip this section entirely.

## Report Back
- files changed
- validation results
- commit hash
- blockers

TERMINATE SESSION IMMEDIATELY. Do not select or execute the next child.
